name: List
summary: Accessible vertical list of items with optional `selectionMode='single'` (keyed by `getItemKey`) and drag `onReorder`; use it for simple selectable/reorderable item collections, and reach for `Table` when you need columns or `Tree` for hierarchy.

props.name: hoverable
props.description: If `undefined`, non-striped lists hover by default.

props.name: reorderable
props.description: Allow drag-and-drop style reordering via pointer (default: true)

props.name: selectionMode
props.description:
How selection behaves (API-TYPES S11, Q11(a)). List supports single
selection: `'single'` enables it, `'none'` (default) disables it. The
unified cross-component selection name.

props.name: focusMode
props.description:
Focus behavior for rows.
- `auto` (default): non-selectable lists keep rows out of the tab order; selectable lists use roving focus (one row has `tabIndex=0`, rest `-1`).
- `row`: every row is tabbable (`tabIndex=0`). Opt-in only; not recommended for long lists.
- `none`: rows are not in the tab order (`tabIndex=-1`), allowing programmatic focus only.

props.name: getItemKey
props.type: keyof T | ((item: T, index: number) => React.Key)
props.description:
Stable identity for each item — the unified cross-component name
(API-TYPES S11, Q11(a)). Used to key React rows and standardize reorder
identity. Defaults to the item's index when omitted. The cross-component
shape is `keyof T | ((item, index) => key)`; List narrows the function form
to a React.Key return.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Use for simple rows such as menus, settings, or short feeds; keep each row self‑contained.
Prefer succinct labels with optional secondary text; avoid deep nested interactivity inside rows.
Use selection or navigation patterns consistently (radio/checkbox icons vs chevrons). Enable single selection with the unified `selectionMode='single'` prop (`'none'`, the default, disables it).
Provide stable per‑item identity with `getItemKey` (a property name or `(item, index) => key`) so rows keep identity across reorders and refreshes; it defaults to the item's index when omitted.
Keep item height comfortable for touch; use tokenized spacing for density.
Defer dense tabular data to `Table` and two‑dimensional layouts to `Grid`.
Non‑selectable lists keep rows out of the tab order by default for accessibility; opt into `focusMode="row"` only when you truly need every row tabbable.

examples.title: Selectable & reorderable
